name: Ecological adoption comparison
research_question: When a developer adopts Lamina versus using Plan mode and implementing (the realistic without-Lamina path), which produces better implemented product behavior in code?

control.label: Plan mode + implement
control.description: Write bench-plan.md (IDE Plan-mode-style planning), then implement a minimal vertical slice. No Lamina skills. No verify/fix loop.
control.precedent: Matches the without-Lamina arm of the public hotel-booking demo (README.md): Cursor Plan mode → implement.

treatment.label: Full Lamina loop
treatment.description: lamina-init → lamina-design or lamina-verify (audit) → implement → lamina-verify → fix from fix.md

why_unequal_turns_is_correct: The benchmark answers an adoption question: should I install Lamina, not a tooling-ablation question inside a process nobody would use without Lamina.
Extra treatment phases (verify, fix) are the product — not hidden compute. Scoring treatment post-fix measures what Lamina is designed to deliver.
Forcing the control through a Lamina-shaped verify/fix loop would import Lamina's methodology into the baseline and understate the real without-Lamina path.
Matched-turn designs measure whether slash commands beat manual copies of Lamina's loop — a narrower claim than this benchmark makes.
fairness_constraints: Same coding agent, model pin, temperature, task prompt, and fixture for both arms on each task.
Control receives no Lamina skills and no .lamina/ contracts.
Both arms score the same artifact type: bundled application source (process markdown and .lamina/ excluded).
Control is scored at its natural stopping point (post-implement); treatment at its natural stopping point (post-fix). This is intentional, not a scoring shortcut.
explicit_non_claims: Does not claim matched compute fairness — ecological validity is prioritized.
Does not claim runtime/test pass rates unless behavior probes include trusted shell/runtime oracles (reported separately from the composite).
Does not claim model independence from a single agent pin.
Human review is optional qualitative only — not part of the claim composite.

rejected_alternatives.why_rejected: Exports Lamina's design→verify→fix loop into the control arm. Tests tooling inside Lamina's process, not Lamina vs realistic baseline.
Task→implement with no planning understates what competent teams do without Lamina; Plan+implement is the documented comparison in the product demo.
